programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: In Mexico, the crew navigate a maze of crocodile and mosquito-infested waterways and lagoons in search of a rumoured paradise for fishing tarpon

programme.title: Project Grizzly
programme.sub-title: To Teach a Bear to Fish
programme.desc: Jeff Watson tries to teach his rescued grizzly bears Bob and Screech how to fish - but it proves more difficult than imagined and he appears to be falling behind

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: Mireyas darkest nightmare comes true, and Russell investigates a possible Bigfoot underwater passageway

programme.title: Tiger Shark King

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Kristen, whose cat Moose makes a misery of her life by marking everything in her house. The expert works his techniques to transform its ways

programme.title: Must Love Cats
programme.desc: John Fulton heads to the American southwest to meet felines with frontier flair, and learns that everything is bigger in Texas

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: On the Gulf of Mexico, a routine inspection turns treacherous for Carmen Rickel and the Marine Tactical Operations Group. Kyle Hendley wrangles a rattlesnake

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff fights to save a bearded dragon that is having a seizure, and Dr Amy is put to the test when a routine cat neuter turns complicated

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff must act fast to save a kitten with head trauma, while Dr Amy treats a dog with a weak heart. A passer-by helps a family when their dog is hit by a car

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Tia comes to the rescue of five injured dogs who have been used for fighting. Parolee Spencer is released from jail and returns to Villalobos

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: When a litter of puppies are dropped off at Villalobos, Tia and M2 turn to social media to get them adopted. Mariah and Lizzy struggle to catch a stray

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: In a first for the show, Jackson Galaxy helps a cat that cannot get along in its rescue home to move in with a foster family, hoping they will eventually adopt the fussy feline

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson comes to the aid of room-mates Josh and Grace, who cannot keep their cats from attacking each other. He also helps a mother and daughter struggling with a fearful feral cat

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson builds a treehouse for the Mt McKinley Princess Wilderness Lodge, an A-frame construction allowing views of the Alaskan mountains

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: A year in the life of five slow-moving residents of the Aviarios Sloth Sanctuary in Costa Rica, a place dedicated to saving orphaned or injured sloths. Buttercup gets a new throne, a romance develops between two three-toed bradypuses, and the resident scientist gets to the bottom of the ugly business of sloth digestion

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: An emergency rescue puts sanctuary vet Marcello on the spot, a love triangle leaves bradypus Samantha marginalised, and the resident scientist puts sloth speed to the test

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: An experiment to find out if wild wolves are smarter than domestic dogs. Plus, how polar bears keep warm and whether spiders can fly

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Fun facts about animals, asking why drowned ants do not drown, whether woodpeckers get headaches and how a 15ft octopus can escape through a three-inch hole

programme.title: The Diamond Collar
programme.desc: Former mob street enforcer James Giuliani helps to rehome abused and abandoned animals in Brooklyn, New York. He begins by hiring some help

programme.title: The Diamond Collar
programme.desc: James Giuliani receives a call from an old mafia acquaintance about a rescue at a junkyard. His assistant shows up for her first day as a groomer, but is soon overawed

programme.title: Alaska: Surviving the Last Frontier
programme.desc: Carolyn Wise heads out on a moose hunt and the Flower Lake group stalks squirrels. The other groups are forced to take desperate measures to find food

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Brett Raymer and Wayde King recall some of their favourite moments from the first series, and begin work on a custom tank for a restaurant on Coney Island

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Brothers-in-law Brett Raymer and Wayde King recreate a spine insert for an aquarium, before building an underwater forest for a doctors office in southern California

programme.title: Must Love Cats
programme.desc: John Fulton discovers how the comic-strip cat Garfield was born, what cat litter is made of, and whether a specimen with four ears has better than the average hearing

programme.title: Sharks of the Shadowlands

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: The team believe they have finally found where Bigfoot has been hiding throughout their expedition, and bring in a high-tech drone to investigate

programme.desc: A crippling fear of bats prevents Tom from leaving the house at night, Roxanne will not enter the bathroom by herself for fear of rats, and Laura strives hard to avoid cockroaches

programme.title: Preposterous Pets
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on people who keep all manner of animals as pets. In the first edition, a so-called lion whisperer in the Czech Republic talks about his big cats. Plus, the story of a Texan cowboy who lets buffalo roam in his house

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: A call from NBA star Dwight Howard sends the duo to Houston, but the basketball player does not just want a fish tank, he also wants a new home for his snakes

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: Field operations manager DaBolt and investigator Barkley respond to reports of an illegal dog fight and discover three pitbulls urgently need their help

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: The investigators look back on cases where they made the difference between life and death, including a dog found tied up and left in a skip

programme.title: Must Love Cats
programme.desc: John Fulton discovers how the comic-strip cat Garfield was born, what cat litter is made of, and whether a specimen with four ears has better than the average hearing

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Earl helps restore an abandoned rottweiler to health. A service dog organisation from North Carolina contacts the centre seeking a new dog for their programme

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy enters the Sante Dor shelter with the intention of helping an over-aggressive shelter cat who goes by the name of Pig Pen

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps the owners of Scout, who is so afraid of his tormentor Caramel that he lives inside a kitchen cabinet

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: The Game Wardens have a busy weekend in Texas as they deal with thousands of festival-goers on the San Marcos River and a patrol on Lake Texoma leads to multiple arrests

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff operates on a rottweiler with a broken shoulder and Dr Baier sees to a limping pig. The team heads to the mountains to treat some wolves

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson creates an off-the-grid escape that is all about peace, love, and treehouses. Once built, the owner adds a massive hammock in the trees

programme.title: Sharks of the Shadowlands
programme.desc: Attack survivor Jenny Oliver and researcher Kina Scollay set out to determine whether sevengill sharks are targeting divers, and changing behavioural patterns to hunt in packs

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: The Kilchers face the threat of climate change, and after another winter of record temperatures, life on the homestead appears to be changing and everything is in flux

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: Bear stays in the woods overnight after learning some life-changing news. Elsewhere, Sister Bird makes an unusual gift for Gabe, and Bam works on the house

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: A family whose appeal for help with strange paranormal events in their home was dismissed, leading them to call in the paranormal team

programme.title: Must Love Cats
programme.desc: Exploring the birthplace of the domestic shorthair. Plus, a look at how many breeds compete at Cat Fanciers Association-sponsored shows, and the prime feline task in New York

programme.title: My Extreme Animal Phobia
programme.desc: Marvin is reduced to tears by the sight of a pit bull puppy, an encounter with a snake makes Seth a prisoner in his own home and arachnophobe Jahara refuses to open any windows

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: A friend asks Pete Nelson to build him a treehouse strictly for fun and relaxation, so he creates a space complete with a wine bar, loft and even a hot tub

programme.title: Animal Cops Miami
programme.desc: Investigators resolve to get justice for a neglected dog and its puppy, and agent Casadevall helps members of the canine population suffering in the Miami heat

programme.title: Animal Cops Miami
programme.desc: Investigators find a puppy suffering without water or shelter, and a tourist attraction has an unwanted visitor - an alligator

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps a man with Crohns disease who is worried about his cats constant moaning, and also diagnoses the problems of an old-age feline

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: The natural world at rest offers a constant source of calm and comfort, helping people to experience more being and less doing. Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe talks viewers through the process and onto an immersive journey around the sights and sounds of resting wildlife all over the planet

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.desc: The presenter tells the story of the 17th-century residence in Derbyshire - the pleasure palace of playboy Cavalier and ambitious courtier William Cavendish. Through a collection of artworks and items held within the castle, Lucy uncovers a colourful tale, capturing the tensions of England in the 1600s that would eventually lead the nation to civil war

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A concerned mother hires Hetty to find out who is stalking her daughter just days before her wedding. Its not long before the unlikely sleuth is on the trail of a disgraced soldier with a grudge against the father of the bride-to-be. Light-hearted drama, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs hosts a 1977 edition of the old-time music hall programme, with Val Doonican, Sheila Steafel, Rita Morris and Dailey and Wayne entertaining an audience from the stage of the Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Europe, a crowded continent hiding the most surprising animals in pockets of wilderness. Gibraltar Nature Reserve is home to Europes only wild population of primates, with its Barbary macaques living a life of kidnapping and high drama, while in the cemeteries of Vienna graverobbing European hamsters do battle with one another. Italian mountain villages are the hunting grounds for rarely seen wolves, and lynx lurk in the forests of Spain

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.desc: The presenter tells the story of the 17th-century residence in Derbyshire - the pleasure palace of playboy Cavalier and ambitious courtier William Cavendish. Through a collection of artworks and items held within the castle, Lucy uncovers a colourful tale, capturing the tensions of England in the 1600s that would eventually lead the nation to civil war

programme.title: Inside Classical
programme.desc: Georgia Mann presents as Sakari Oramo conducts the BBC Symphony Orchestra in a nature-inspired celebration of orchestral colour at Londons Barbican Hall. Igor Stravinskys 1945 concert suite The Firebird opens the programme with magic and folklore in this masterpiece of vivid contrasts and musical storytelling. After this, Judith Weirs atmospheric work The Welcome Arrival of Rain brings relief and reflection, shaped by the rhythms and moods of the natural world. South Korean pianist Yeol Eum Son adds expressive brilliance and contemplation to Finzis Eclogue for Piano and Strings Op 10 and closes the concert with the warmth and virtuosity of the folk-inspired melodies of Bartoks Third Piano Concerto

programme.title: Welsh Greats
programme.desc: Aled Jones presents a programme exploring the life and career of the comedian and singer, a down-to-earth Swansea lad who became one of the nations best-loved entertainers. From The Goons to Highway, and from Pickwick to Songs of Praise, he was always ready with a giggle and a song, bt his good humour gave no hint of the serious challenges he had faced, from the brutality of war to life-threatening illness

programme.title: Wogan
programme.desc: The actress sits in for Terry Wogan and is joined by Harry Secombe and Spike Milligan. With music by the Alarm, accompanied by the Morriston Orpheus male voice choir. First broadacst in 1989

programme.title: Rachels Farm
programme.desc: Film director and actress Rachel Ward joins a farming revolution. She voyages from willful ignorance about the ecological impacts of conventional agriculture on her own rural property, to embracing a movement to restore the health of Australias farmland, food and climate

programme.title: Organ Stops: Saving the King of Instruments
programme.desc: Documentary following the tireless efforts of a small band of enthusiasts to save, restore and rehome rare pipe organs that would otherwise be destroyed. Martin Renshaw dedicates his life to travelling the UK on organ rescue missions, sound engineer Marc Ogier rebuilds instruments in his back bedroom from pipes bought online, organ builder Dominic Gwynn resuscitates historical pieces that are close to death, and avant-garde musician Claire Singer uses pipe organs to create a new kind of acoustic sound

programme.title: Chauvet: Humanitys First Great Masterpiece
programme.desc: Following a 30-year archaeological, geological and artistic adventure as scientists endeavour to understand and conserve the art of the Chauvet cave. Hidden deep inside Frances Gorges de lArdèche, the cave was revealed to the modern world for the very first time in 1994. It has over 1,000 paintings adorning its walls and scientists immediately recognised the importance of the discovery

programme.title: Welsh Greats
programme.desc: Aled Jones presents a programme exploring the life and career of the comedian and singer, a down-to-earth Swansea lad who became one of the nations best-loved entertainers. From The Goons to Highway, and from Pickwick to Songs of Praise, he was always ready with a giggle and a song, bt his good humour gave no hint of the serious challenges he had faced, from the brutality of war to life-threatening illness

programme.title: Inside Classical
programme.desc: Georgia Mann presents as Sakari Oramo conducts the BBC Symphony Orchestra in a nature-inspired celebration of orchestral colour at Londons Barbican Hall. Igor Stravinskys 1945 concert suite The Firebird opens the programme with magic and folklore in this masterpiece of vivid contrasts and musical storytelling. After this, Judith Weirs atmospheric work The Welcome Arrival of Rain brings relief and reflection, shaped by the rhythms and moods of the natural world. South Korean pianist Yeol Eum Son adds expressive brilliance and contemplation to Finzis Eclogue for Piano and Strings Op 10 and closes the concert with the warmth and virtuosity of the folk-inspired melodies of Bartoks Third Piano Concerto

programme.title: Wogan
programme.desc: The actress sits in for Terry Wogan and is joined by Harry Secombe and Spike Milligan. With music by the Alarm, accompanied by the Morriston Orpheus male voice choir. First broadacst in 1989

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke concludes the documentary by revealing how the decline of the Egyptian empire ran parallel with a creative revival that produced, among other proclamations of the pharaohs power, colossal statues of Ramses II. He then discovers that invasions by foreign powers such as the Nubians and the Romans led to the production of hybrid forms of art, before revealing a selection of satirical work that continues to inspire Egypts graffiti artists today

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Robert Robinson presides over another duel of words and wits as Frank Muir, Gayle Hunnicutt and Russell Davies take on Patrick Campbell, Prunella Scales and Paul Eddington. First broadcast in 1977

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Bernard Levin and Robin Ray. With guest musician Gerald Moore. First broadcast in 1973

programme.title: Caroline Walker: Womens Work
programme.desc: Documentary following the Internationally renowned Scottish artist over four years as she makes large-scale paintings representing the unseen, often undervalued aspects of womens labour, from hospitals to hotels, nail bars to nursery settings. As the film follows Walker through pregnancy and the birth of her second child, it provides an exploration of the role of the artist mother, until recently viewed as taboo in contemporary art creativity.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: An alternative history of the British Isles told through 1,500 years of art, with leading creatives exploring key cultural works that have shaped British art and culture. This first edition examines the turbulent era that followed the Roman occupation of Britain, with sculptor Antony Gormley examining Spong Man, a clay figure that once sat on a fifth-century funerary urn, and actor Michael Sheen performing Y Gododdin, a seventh-century Welsh poem of resistance against the Anglo-Saxons

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Bernard Levin and Robin Ray. With guest musician Gerald Moore. First broadcast in 1973

programme.title: Caroline Walker: Womens Work
programme.desc: Documentary following the Internationally renowned Scottish artist over four years as she makes large-scale paintings representing the unseen, often undervalued aspects of womens labour, from hospitals to hotels, nail bars to nursery settings. As the film follows Walker through pregnancy and the birth of her second child, it provides an exploration of the role of the artist mother, until recently viewed as taboo in contemporary art creativity.

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke concludes the documentary by revealing how the decline of the Egyptian empire ran parallel with a creative revival that produced, among other proclamations of the pharaohs power, colossal statues of Ramses II. He then discovers that invasions by foreign powers such as the Nubians and the Romans led to the production of hybrid forms of art, before revealing a selection of satirical work that continues to inspire Egypts graffiti artists today

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by actor James McAvoy, who talks about his directorial debut, The Morning Show star Gugu Mbatha-Raw, discussing her crime thriller Fuze, actress, singer and producer Rita Wilson, chatting about her sixth studio album, and comedian Russell Howard, whos promoting his stand-up tour Dont Tell the Algorithm

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Harry Clark Goes to Rome
programme.desc: The Traitors star goes on a deeply personal journey from Slough to Rome and Vatican City in search of answers about belief, identity and what it means to be a good Catholic in the modern world. Along the way, hes challenged on his lapsed habits, engages in traditional pilgrim practices and raises questions about the role of faith in modern Britain

programme.title: Easter Day Service - Live from Ripon Cathedral
programme.desc: Coverage of the Festal Eucharist on Easter Sunday from Ripon Cathedral in North Yorkshire, led by the Dean of Ripon, the Very Rev John Dobson. In her sermon, the Bishop of Ripon, the Rt Rev Anna Eltringham, reflects on the women being the first disciples to discover the empty tomb and encounter the risen Christ.

programme.title: Urbi et Orbi
programme.desc: The Popes traditional Easter greeting and blessing from St Peters Square

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Owain Wyn Evans oversee three projects in Kent, Yorkshire and London in which timescales are thrown out the window

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell helps the teams at Peterborough Festival of Antiques and presenter Eric Knowles has a mystery item for fair-goers to identify

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Pam Rhodes introduces favourite Easter hymns and joins Christians in Cornwall as they mark 50 years of raising the 30-foot Carn Brea Cross on a rocky outcrop for Holy Week

programme.title: Attenboroughs Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the extraordinary ways that some animals use colour to hide and disappear into the background. The Bengal tigers of central India use their striped patterns to dazzle colour blind prey, while zebras on the Masai Mara use motion dazzle to confuse predators. Other animals use colour to trick and to deceive. On Australias Great Barrier Reef, a blue-striped blenny uses colours to mimic other fish and launch a sudden attack

programme.title: Crookhaven
programme.desc: A spy wreaks havoc in the school, Gabriels search for his parents leads him into the heart of Crookhaven, and Edgar will stop at nothing to win the shilling test

programme.title: Crookhaven
programme.desc: The Crooklings are back home and breaking school rules, Amira and Nicki lock horns as Penelope demands answers, and a real-world heist brings the gang face-to-face with the enemy

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: Easter: The Sights and Sounds of Spring
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit Bisterne Estate in Hampshires Avon Valley on Easter Sunday, where farmland and water meadows provide a vital home for lapwings and hares. Plus, author Robert Macfarlane shares an exclusive spring reading, and on his Cotswolds farm, Adam Henson restores a traditional hedgerow, just in time for nesting birds

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the hidden wild world of Britains backyards. The first episode focuses on the garden of a thousand-year-old mill house in Oxfordshire. As the winter floods recede, a kingfisher searches for a mate, a vole must evade a range of dangers, and otters and red kites patrol the river, both day and night

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Belfasts Botanic Gardens. Treasures include a collection of football shirts worn by some of the worlds most famous goalkeepers, a trio of brooches, a modernist silver tea set, and a seascape depicting the city on a rainy day. Fiona also discovers the drawings and research of unsung Irish botanist John Templeton, and art expert Grant Ford shares works by his favourite Irish artists

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Under pressure from her mother, Mary weighs up an unconventional offer from Mr Ryder. As she grapples with what she truly wants, Lizzy and Mr Collins help her to chart her own course. But just as she thinks shes taken her future into her own hands, a surprise visit from her aunt and uncle leaves everyone reeling

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary joins the Gardiners in the Lake District, hoping for clarity and calm. She reconnects with Mr Hayward, and they bond over poetry and nature. But just as it seems as though they might finally declare their feelings for one another, Mr Ryder arrives to disrupt proceedings, with Caroline Bingley in tow

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Carey and Pierson escape into a network of underground tunnels. Unarmed and with no back-up, Carey is horrified by what Pierson has been through, and the extent of the conspiracy that they are both now entangled in. Simons real identity is revealed as allies above ground piece together the truth, and Carey forges an uneasy alliance to bring down the forces truly responsible for Isaac Turners murder

programme.title: The Hunger Games: The Ballad of Songbirds and Snakes (2023)
programme.desc: Coriolanus Snow, a young man who will go on to become the tyrannical ruler of the Panem civilisation, is assigned to mentor a contender in the 10th ritual games. He finds himself torn between his feelings for his protegee and the opportunity to turn his familys failing fortunes around. Sci-fi adventure prequel, with Tom Blyth, Rachel Zegler, Viola Davis and Peter Dinklage

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary and Angelina Bakalarou work together to repair a lamp left to visitor John by his friend Brian who passed away during the Aids epidemic. He has fond memories of the lamp decorating Brians flat during his lavish cocktail parties in the 1980s and is hoping the experts can bring it back to its former glory. Chris from Surrey brings in his father Chriss shoes used when he was a training partner to Roger Bannister when he broke the four-minute mile. Sisters Sara and Simone bring in a brooch left to them by their step-grandmother Jean who died in a plane crash in 1973 for Richard Talman to take a look at. Mark Stuckey is tasked with repairing a reel-to-reel audio recorder that holds performances by visitor Michaels father from before he died

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble provide an update on Bart the capybara as he is introduced to a new mate. Plus, Kate heads to Koala Creek to be introduced to a bettong and a long-nosed potoroo, two species that have been badly affected by the Australian wild fires but now play a vital role in the recovery of those forests

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: In Co Durham, reporter Paul Colgan investigates the enduring crime of livestock rustling. He meets a farmer whose stolen animals cost him thousands and observes officers trialling a bold new operation to stop thieves in their tracks. In East Yorkshire, metal detectorists are suspected of stealing historic treasures under the cover of darkness, while in Hertfordshire, farmers and police fight back against illegal fly-tipping on farmland - a problem reaching epidemic proportions, choking the countryside and leaving landowners to foot the bill

programme.title: SAS: Catching the Criminals
programme.desc: With shoplifting gangs are on the rise, ex-SAS leader Billy Billingham and Cheshire Police use facial recognition powered by AI to catch suspected criminals

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Celebrity special, featuring Carol Smillie, who is in for a shock when she sees the state of the property Martin Roberts shows her around in Scotland. Plus, a look back at a 2006 episode, when Martin saw a house in a stunning location in Wiltshire, and Jacqui Joseph was in Warwickshire, where outside space was at a premium

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two families go head-to-head at a Staffordshire antiques fair, aided by John Cameron and Irita Marriott, and Natasha Raskin Sharp indulges her passion for Arts and Crafts jewellery

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Roman Kemp meets a Glasgow childrens entertainer who is desperate for a good nights sleep. Adding a hot bath to an evening routine has been shown to help improve sleep, reduce the risk of heart disease and even burn a few extra calories

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Amelia Bouquet convert a bland plot in Scunthorpe, while Lee Burkhill and Flo Headlam are in Doncaster to create a Eurovision-winning garden

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps one family, spread over four generations, to find two houses in one village, including a mystery house that promises to bring them very close together

programme.title: Peter Rabbit (2018)
programme.desc: A mischievous rabbit has the run of a farm and its vegetable patch when the owner unexpectedly dies. When a relative inherits the property, he strives to rid his land of the rabbit and his woodland friends - but his feelings for a nature-loving woman complicate the battle of wits with the animal kingdom. Family comedy based on Beatrix Potters stories, starring Domhnall Gleeson and Rose Byrne, with the voices of James Corden and Margot Robbie

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Tom Allen on the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah assists entrepreneur and former Apprentice winner Tim Campbell and his wife Jas, who are keen to leave urban life behind. Homing in on rural Essex, the couple wish to swap sales pitches for sail power as they take to the water in-between touring an exceptional range of country properties, on the hunt for a family home that meets their needs

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan travels north for the next stop of his Wild Atlantic journey. He explores the north west coast with his friend, the artist Terry Bradley. He also does some fossil hunting to find the materials to make some extraordinary jewellery, and he doesnt miss a chance to get on stage to duet with singer Claudia Buckley in one of Donegals buzzing country music venues

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein lead the search with guest judge and passionate gardener Penny Lancaster to find Northern Irelands Greatest Garden. The judges face a difficult decision when it comes to choosing between Ivydene Garden near Comber, a tropical backyard over in Lisburn, and a rock garden in Ballynahinch

programme.title: Chronicles of the Sperrins
programme.desc: This episode celebrates Autumn. Sean and his guide dog are climbing Bessy Bell, and following the footsteps of many to the Holy well whose waters he believes have the power to heal. Lynne and her pilot husband enjoy the Sperrins from the air, and Halloween celebrations are in full swing at Butterlope Farm

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jasmine receives a visitor, Nicola is left frustrated, and Ross and Harvey hatch a plan

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Flashbacks show Stephen and Lisas first meeting, while Amandas pregnancy and Daves ambitions create uncertainty for all

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Roy Wood Jr chairs the satirical current affairs quiz, with Armando Iannucci and Anushka Asthana joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the recent headlines

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: In Halifax, Kanada and Chris help a farmer who crashed into a dry-stone wall while on his way to feed his animals and in Sheffield, another farmer is having memory loss after his vehicle overturned into a roadside ditch. The team grapple with an influx of calls leaving patients waiting for ambulances

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mobeen Azhars investigation into prison corruption uncovers the murder of a former officer who had been involved in exposing an illicit relationship. When news breaks of a prison governor being jailed for a relationship with a drug-dealing gang boss, Mobeen becomes determined to uncover the true extent of criminal influence inside our prisons

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny offers to provide some under-age kids with alcohol, but having been banned from the off-licence, it falls to Louise to provide the goods. When she returns empty-handed, all hell breaks loose. Donna decides she needs to find new friends, while Gaz goes in search of casual sex

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: The conflicts between the group conspire to ruin Janet and Jonnys hen and stag parties. The plan for a quiet baby shower rather than a raucous girls night out goes awry, as Donna refuses to come and Louise is desperate to let her hair down before she gets sent to jail

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: Documentary following the British-Gambian musician as he attempts to rebuild his life and career after prison, returning to live performance while still on probation, navigating curfews, travel restrictions and the risk of recall. He makes his comeback at the Glastonbury Festival, but touring in Europe hangs in the balance, and he faces a choice between breaking commitments and risking a return to prison

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May are challenged to create a TV advert for Volkswagen, but soon fall out as James concentrates on detailed information on fuel economy and Jeremy wants something more exciting. Richard Hammond explores an alternative to Germanic sports saloons and tests the Vauxhall VXR8 Bathurst, and former US chat show host Jay Leno is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Press X to Continue
programme.sub-title: What Do NFTs and the Blockchain Mean for Gaming?
programme.desc: BBC gaming reporter Steffan Powell chats about forgotten titles, contemplating what NFTs and the blockchain mean for gaming

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Penny and Vinny have their first baby scan, Zoe tries to build bridges and Zack faces financial problems

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Josh puts his best foot forward, Penny weighs up her options and Vinnys plans cause a stir

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Elaine and Ian remain at odds, Penny is given a choice and the Truemans worry about Patrick

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The residents gather for the opening of Knight Fusion, Ian attempts to make amends, and Vicki and Ross think about wedding venues

programme.title: The Proposal (2009)
programme.desc: A Canadian publishing editor working in New York faces deportation to her own country. Desperate to stay in America, she offers her assistant a promotion if he will marry her, but an immigration official becomes suspicious and the pair are forced to go to desperate lengths to look like a happy couple. Romantic comedy, starring Sandra Bullock and Ryan Reynolds

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: The team travel to Milton Keynes to help the Parej family, who have five children under nine, transform their cramped and chaotic home. The family tackle an overflow of toys, clothes, storage cubes and some strong emotions as they work to get past their what if mentality and make a manageable home that works for them

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. After a video featuring a female prison officer and an inmate having sex inside HMP Wandsworth goes viral, journalist Mobeen Azhar begins an investigation into what happened and why. A year of twists, turns and damning revelations pulls back the curtain on a dangerous wider world of coercion, corruption and contraband thriving behind bars

programme.title: Missing in Paradise: Searching for Sarm
programme.desc: Journalist Tir Dhondy investigates the disappearance of Sarm Heslop, who went missing from a luxury Caribbean charter yacht in 2021. Tire retraces Sarms last known movements and tracks down two boat captains whose vessels were moored near the yacht she vanished from. Also, she gets to question Ryan Banes, Sarms boyfriend, account of what happened what happened through his lawyer David Cattie

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring magazine

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni investigates how Sean Combs has gone from being the worlds biggest hip-hop mogul to haemorrhaging money, businesses, friends and fans in a matter of months. Yinka will examine how Combs made it to the top, and what some may have turned a blind eye to along the way. Speaking to some of Combs inner circle, and biggest critics, Yinka will find out what they think about him and ask why so many are only speaking out now

programme.title: Press X to Continue
programme.sub-title: Are the Latest Consoles Any Good?
programme.desc: Steffan Powell, the BBCs gaming reporter, and the Press X gang talk next-generation gaming

programme.title: Blood, Sweat and T-Shirts
programme.desc: The six youngsters experience the physical challenge of the cotton harvest, where they are shocked by the laborious process and the appalling standard of the living conditions, with the daily wage being just £1.20. Though they are in a dingy hovel with no amenities, for Georgina the most horrifying fact is that her Indian co-workers shop for clothes twice a year at most

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall finds out about deadly animals in the UK, discovering dragonflies are not only skilled killers in the air, but also terrorise the underwater world as larvae

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team head to Alaska, where they spot humpback whales and grizzly bears

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring magazine

programme.title: Harry Clark Goes to Rome
programme.desc: The Traitors star goes on a deeply personal journey from Slough to Rome and Vatican City in search of answers about belief, identity and what it means to be a good Catholic in the modern world. Along the way, hes challenged on his lapsed habits, engages in traditional pilgrim practices and raises questions about the role of faith in modern Britain

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Surviving Americas Most Hated Family
programme.desc: Thirteen years since first encountering one of Americas most notorious hate groups, award-winning film-maker Louis Theroux makes a long-anticipated return to Kansas to spend time with the Westboro Baptist Church - a hugely-controversial Christian ministry that for years has picketed at military funerals and other high-profile events with deliberately provocative and homophobic placards. Immersing himself in the strange world of Westboro, Louis explores what happens when a hate-group largely populated by one family loses its patriarch

programme.title: Let It Snow (2020)
programme.desc: After sneaking onto a restricted slope and becoming separated from her fiance, free-riding snowboarder Mia must survive not only against nature, but the masked snowmobile rider in black who is out for her blood. Horror, starring Ivanna Sakhno and Tinatin Dalakishvili. In Georgian and English

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: The students are teamed up with career mentors, but things quickly go wrong. Leah is fuming with Felix about the sabotage, and Femis dreams are crushed. Omar is in denial, and Toby is distracted by Abbys dangerous games. Violence erupts on the hockey pitch, and the students bright futures feel further away than ever

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Surviving Americas Most Hated Family
programme.desc: Thirteen years since first encountering one of Americas most notorious hate groups, award-winning film-maker Louis Theroux makes a long-anticipated return to Kansas to spend time with the Westboro Baptist Church - a hugely-controversial Christian ministry that for years has picketed at military funerals and other high-profile events with deliberately provocative and homophobic placards. Immersing himself in the strange world of Westboro, Louis explores what happens when a hate-group largely populated by one family loses its patriarch

programme.title: Blood, Sweat and T-Shirts
programme.desc: The teenagers experience life in the Mumbai city slums as they are set to work in segregated sweatshops barely a stones throw from the enticing glamour of Bollywood. One saving grace is that unlike the locals, they do not have to sleep at their sewing machines, but with children below the legal minimum working age with them, they finally agree that something has to be done

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Carey is under pressure to charge James Whitlock. Her failure to reach Napier leads to her facing the consequences of her actions, but an unlikely group of allies presents itself. Pierson must convince his colleagues that he is fit for the job, and an off-the-record military group seek advice from the mysterious Simon on how to get their plans back on track

programme.title: Clash of the Superpowers: America vs China
programme.desc: Part two of two. Xi Jinpings government have high hopes for Joe Biden, Donald Trumps successor as US president, but these are dashed after a frosty meeting in Alaska. Biden sparks further anger in Beijing when he signs a nuclear submarine deal with Australia and the UK, and takes on China over tech, before Nancy Pelosis visit to Taiwan brings the two superpowers closer than ever to military confrontation

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.sub-title: Colourful Upcycling and Traditional Stone Carving
programme.desc: Upcycler Jaycee from West Sussex and stone carver Tristan from Perthshire attend a life-changing training camp in eight acres of ornamental gardens in Oxfordshire, aiming to turn their talents into thriving businesses. Mentored by Dom Chinea and a team of successful artisans and makers, they tackle three making challenges designed to assess their skill and money-making potential

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at themes beginning with the letter B, as part of her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBCs most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Take a Hike
programme.desc: Opera singer Tony, whos lived near Inverness for the past 16 years, leads a musical hike along Cromartys coastal path, in the second Highlands and Islands walk. Although known as a fishing port, Cromarty harbour has been repurposed to repair oil rigs, and the combination of uncompromising hunks of steel and the rugged coastline makes for an unusual skyline. Narrated by Rhod Gilbert

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: St Michaels Mount: After the Storm
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Anita Rani head to St Michaels Mount in Cornwall after Storm Goretti. Hurricane-force winds ripped through the island, leaving the tiny community of just 33 residents facing a huge clean-up. With Easter approaching, they join the race to repair the damage, restore the landscape and reopen this iconic landmark to visitors. Plus, Datshiane Navanayagam investigates the rise of youth homelessness in rural Britain

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: The rural affairs series returns with a special episode to celebrate the programmes 50th anniversary, with Dougie Vipond revisiting stories from the archives and revealing huge changes in the Scottish countryside. Dougie also meets up with past and present members of the Landward team as they share their best moments working on the programme

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: The team returns to offer more horticultural tips and advice, with Carole Baxter and Brian Cunningham kicking off the season with a range of jobs that can be done now. Carole also planting up an Easter container that will bring colour and joy to any garden, while Brian starts some much needed maintenance-pruning on the stem bank border. Plus, a trip to Edinburgh to catch up with what George Anderson is doing in his own garden in Joppa

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Highlights from throughout the series, with Easter recipes from Richard Bainbridge, Judy Joo, Emily Scott and Hasan Semay, while Mel Giedroyc tucks into her food heaven or hell

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Northern Ireland must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again, and only one can go through to the final. They need to impress a panel of exacting judges - Michelin-starred chef Tom Kerridge, former Great British Menu Champion of Champions Lorna McNee, and comedian and all-round food enthusiast Phil Wang. The guest judge is award-winning Belfast director Lisa Barros DSa, whose films Ordinary Love and Good Vibrations have already been celebrated in dishes earlier in the week

programme.title: Easter Parade (1948)
programme.desc: A dancer abandoned by his usual partner trains a chorus girl as a replacement. The new pairing is a great success but, although he strives to keep their relationship strictly professional, he begins to fall head over heels in love with her. Musical, with Fred Astaire, Judy Garland, Peter Lawford and Ann Miller. Songs include A Couple of Swells, Stepping Out with My Baby and Shaking the Blues Away

programme.title: High Society (1956)
programme.desc: Romantic complications arise when a playboy composer sets out to win back his ex-wife. However, she is about to marry another man - and the situation becomes even more difficult when a cynical reporter enters the fray. Musical comedy based on The Philadelphia Story with a score by Cole Porter, starring Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Grace Kelly, with Louis Armstrong appearing as himself

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following Baghani, a tigress chosen to breed in the wild. She is airlifted from her home in an Indian park to a new reserve where she encounters Rajore, a male who has also recently been released. The pair are filmed over two years, as they fight leopards for territory and learn to hunt boar, while trying to avoid being killed by poachers

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of some of the best, unseen finds from the series, visiting the Milestones Museum in Basingstoke Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, the National Maritime Museum Cornwall in Falmouth, and the RAF museum in Hendon, London. Items include an art nouveau necklace and a pair of British railway lamps

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Callum needs to haggle hard to get a good price for his calves from a mainland buyer, while Zaras morning cattle check brings devastating news. Andrew feels under pressure to make the farm pay more so his dad can take a step back, but right now its all hands to the pump as a pregnant Gayle has been signed off work. Noah wants a future in farming, but he needs to balance lambing with exam revision.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Change sweeps across Xudum, and every big cat character must adapt to survive. The core of the Xudum lion pride has moved south, but a group of its young, opportunistic females has remained in the north. The crew is excited to find one of these lionesses has cubs, but as a first-time mum, she has a lot to learn. Cheetah mum Pobe experiences tragedy when a leopard attacks, but hope returns when her three-year-old daughter Neelo is found, and shes thriving, while Lediba the leopard reveals there is more to her than meets the eye, but shes under increasing pressure to catch large prey

programme.title: Burma with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon ventures into Burmas highlands, where he attends a fire balloon festival and visits an elephant sanctuary, where the animals can enjoy a peaceful life after working in the logging industry. He also makes a secret journey into one of the countrys conflict zones to meet members of a rebel army fighting against the Burmese military

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: Diversitys Ashley Banjo, actresses Hermione Norris and Patsy Kensit., reality star Tasha Ghouri; stand-up comedians Hasan Al-Habib and Ashley Blaker and broadcaster Jayne Middlemiss make a journey through the landscapes of north-east England to Lindisfarne, one of Britains most important pilgrimage sites. They trek through the heart of Northumberland and the Scottish Borders along a curated network of trails celebrating three of Englands most revered seventh-century northern English saints - Hild, Oswald and Cuthbert

programme.title: The Imitation Game (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of mathematician Alan Turing, whose work during the Second World War translated seemingly unbreakable Nazi codes as well as pioneering the development of computer technology. In the years following the war, his life would be destroyed as his homosexuality was discovered, leaving him facing criminal charges. Starring Benedict Cumberbatch, Keira Knightley and Charles Dance

programme.title: Being There (1979)
programme.desc: A simple-minded gardener who has spent his life in seclusion ventures into the outside world for the first time after his employer dies. His homespun outlook on life is mistaken for philosophical wisdom and puts him on the road to becoming a highly influential political adviser. Hal Ashbys satirical comedy, based on the novel by Jerzy Kosinski, starring Peter Sellers, Shirley MacLaine and an Oscar-winning Melvyn Douglas

programme.title: Killing Sherlock: Lucy Worsley on the Case of Conan Doyle
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes made his creator Arthur Conan Doyle rich and famous, but the writer came to hate his fictional character. Over the course of three programmes, historian Lucy Worsley investigates this curious relationship between Holmes and Doyle, beginning by examining the sleuths origins in Doyles time as a medical student. She unpicks the early stories, revealing the dark underbelly of late Victorian Britain, from drug use to true crime, and traces Doyles growing disenchantment with his detective, heading to Switzerland to visit the site of one of the most famous deaths in literature.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Return of the programme following investigations into serious crimes. When 12-year-old Leo Ross is fatally stabbed in Birminghams Trittiford Mill Park on his way home from school, police suspect a 14-year-old, but need forensics to prove he is the killer. There is little information about whether Leo was targeted, and finding a murder weapon in such a vast area is an immediate challenge. The park has numerous pathways, an area of marshland, and no CCTV coverage., but bodycam footage from first responders proves to be crucial

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Drama, starring Paapa Essiedu and Siobhán Cullen as a couple dealing with the aftermath of losing a pregnancy. Lisa and Stephen try to get pregnant again, and a dinner exposes hidden tensions

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Potter Emily from London and Nottingham-based textile enthusiast Momoka look to change their lives with the help of Dom Chinea and his team of mentors. After giving up her day job, Emily needs her fledgling pottery business to pay the bills. Mentor Florian is concerned her work doesnt have a consistent sense of style, and thinks thats the key to finding her market. Momoka has a passion for hand-weaving and textiles, but really needs to make her work pay to support her young family. Mentor Piyush worries that shes focusing on the wrong markets and lacks the confidence to sell her pieces

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball prescribes ten minutes of sunshine a day for retired woodworker Neville, who has suffered a series of heart attacks. She explains how just ten minutes of sunlight a day has been shown to improve heart health, strengthen bones and lift mood

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents from Shepton Mallet, Somerset. Mother-and-daughter duo Bernie and Emma search for bargains with the help of Raj Bisram but find themselves reluctant to part with cash at first. On the blue team, friends Amy and Caroline are on the hunt for pottery with the help of Thomas Forrester

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: The team travel to Milton Keynes to help the Parej family, who have five children under nine, transform their cramped and chaotic home. The family tackle an overflow of toys, clothes, storage cubes and some strong emotions as they work to get past their what if mentality and make a manageable home that works for them

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Doctor Zhivago (1965)
programme.desc: A married Russian physician falls in love with another woman, but as their passionate affair plays out against the raging backdrop of the First World War and the Bolshevik revolution, he is torn between his desires and his loyalty to his wife. David Leans epic drama based on Boris Pasternaks novel, starring Omar Sharif, Julie Christie, Geraldine Chaplin, Rod Steiger, Alec Guinness, Ralph Richardson, Tom Courtenay and Klaus Kinski

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britains baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team visits Melksham in Wiltshire, when some of the objects on view include a dagger worn by a Cossack, a drawing by Edmund Dulac, a dog collar made in 1784, a collection of bedpans and a gold box found in the mud in Wales. Presented by Michael Aspel

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Members of the public take their items to be assessed Lydiard Park in Wiltshire, while Paul Martin finds out about the history of the venue

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Michael Ball, Jodie Prenger, Laurence Rickard and Snoochie Shy test their general knowledge skills in a variety of fun trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: The singer heads back to Ireland to reconnect with family and old friends, explore the dramatic western coastline and experience the countrys unique culture, beginning in the county of Cork He meets up with his nephew Ruairí and together they face off in the ancient sport of road bowling along the back roads of the port town of Castletown Berehaven. Ronan also forages for edible plants in a rewilding project area and visits the countrys first Tibetan Buddhist Temple and Retreat Centre on the Beara Peninsula

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Investigations from BBC correspondents all over the world, revealing secrets, rooting out injustice and exposing crime, corruption and abuse

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.sub-title: Iron Gran: Im in My 80s and Doing Triathalons
programme.desc: The comedian invites artist Caroline Pool to paint Eddie - aka Iron Gran - an inspiring 82-year-old who has completed eight Ironman triathlons. After losing her husband in her 50s, Eddie started running long distance to help her cope. At 60 she learned how to swim and at 72, she became the oldest British woman to ever complete an Ironman triathalon, 10-years later she shows no signs of slowing down. Caroline faces the dilemma, should she capture Eddies fun-loving energy or reveal something quieter and more reflective beneath the surface?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie hosts the penultimate semi-final, asking questions on the specialist subjects of Yellowstone National Park, JRR Tolkiens The Silmarillion, Irish pirate Grace OMalley and the England mens team at the FIFA World Cup. The contestants are then tested on their general knowledge before the winner is revealed

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Edinburgh take on Darwin College, Cambridge in the first of the semi-finals, hoping to take one step closer to lifting the series trophy. Amol Rajan asks the questions

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: The celebrities continue their adventure in Durham and visit the 12th-century Norman cathedral, where Canon Charlie Allen introduces them to the ancient pilgrim tradition of foot washing. They then head for Heavenfield, an ancient battlefield, and Housesteads near Hadrians Wal, the UKs best-preserved Roman fort

programme.title: Gosford Park (2001)
programme.desc: A weekend shooting party at a country estate is disrupted by a murder. As the numerous guests deal with their own personal agendas, their actions are quietly observed by their servants, notably an inquisitive maid, who drink in the scandalous gossip while maintaining their own strict social hierarchy. Robert Altmans period drama, starring Maggie Smith, Helen Mirren, Kelly Macdonald, Michael Gambon, Clive Owen and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The teams are unleashed on the pet industry as they are tasked with designing a product and social media campaign to pitch to some of the UKs biggest retailers. With one candidate in the firing line, its a battle for a place in the final five

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: St Michaels Mount: After the Storm
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Anita Rani head to St Michaels Mount in Cornwall after Storm Goretti. Hurricane-force winds ripped through the island, leaving the tiny community of just 33 residents facing a huge clean-up. With Easter approaching, they join the race to repair the damage, restore the landscape and reopen this iconic landmark to visitors. Plus, Datshiane Navanayagam investigates the rise of youth homelessness in rural Britain

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Reality game show with John Hannah as narrator. Five adventurous teams are pushed to the limit, battling extreme climates and harsh terrain in a 12,000km race from Palermo, Sicily, across Europe and Asia to reach Hatgal in northern Mongolia. Best friends Jo and Kush from Liverpool, siblings Katie and Harrison from Manchester, Molly and her father Andrew, cousins Puja and Roshni and in-laws Mark and Margo take on the challenge

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Lisa tries to stay positive in the face of her fears as she discovers she is pregnant again, while Dave overhears Amanda talking about her ex and wants to know more

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Roman Kemp meets a Glasgow childrens entertainer who is desperate for a good nights sleep. Adding a hot bath to an evening routine has been shown to help improve sleep, reduce the risk of heart disease and even burn a few extra calories

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Jacqui and Frankie from The Traitors
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph recruits The Traitors star Frankie Rowan-Plowden to oversee the auction of a property in Kent and theres a cottage in Cornwall that has taken quirky to a new level. Martin Roberts is in Bristol to see a mid-terraced house

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Fields of Danger as Farmer Is Impaled and Eagles Threaten Highland Flocks
programme.desc: Documentary following the police, fire and ambulance services as they attend emergency situations in rural communities. The East Anglian Air Ambulance races to save a farmer after a freak accident, lambs face attack from birds of prey, and reporter Paul Colgan investigates illegal fly-tipping in Cheshire. Featuring accounts from first responders, community members and farmers at the centre of these emergencies

programme.title: Paranormal: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary the most chilling and unexplainable paranormal activity footage captured by eyewitnesses. Children across North America interact with ghosts that their parents cant see and citizens in China fear the worst as hundreds of orbs swarm in the moonlight

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: In Montanas Glacier National Park, some claim that there is a hidden underground Nazi base that may even have been the final hideout for Hitler himself

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: From inexplicable disappearances to mysterious experiments, events in Mount Shasta National Park may be explained by an ancient Karuk Indian legend

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Investigating beliefs held by some experts that many atmospheric light shows are actually caused by extraterrestrials

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: The Toyota Sera is a concept car for the road, with gull-wing doors and a 50s-style space-age glass roof. Its also extremely rare and car enthusiasts Will and Gus manage to find one thats driving - albeit not in the way they need. Is the famed Japanese engineering in place here or have we bought a lemon?

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: Age-old mysterious ruins stand as silent sentinels from the past, with their true history seemingly erased by time. The presenter reveals how modern archaeology is attempting to unravel the enigma of how and why such ancient structures were built

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: During the Middle Ages, the Vikings were infamous for being a bloodthirsty band of seafaring warriors. While much is known about their notorious reputation, historians are still learning surprising new things about this Norse society

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: The men who have had the honour of being President of the United States are celebrated, as esteemed leaders, their names immortalised in the annals of history. The actor looks at many bizarre tales involving US presidents that defy explanation and many years later are still shrouded in mystery

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: Cracking codes has fascinated humanity for centuries. From enigmatic puzzles to secret symbols, the host considers how deciphering historys unbreakable codes may hold answers to mysteries past and present

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: From 1939 until 1945, more than 50 nations engaged in harrowing combat-and during that time, a number of intriguing and persistent mysteries were born. Nearly 80 years later, curious stories about everything from lost gold to secret societies and highly advanced technology remain unexplained. It is hoped that new information can help better understand the strangest tales of the Second World War

programme.title: Banned from the Bible
programme.desc: Part one of two. Religious experts examine the stories that were omitted from the Bible, including the upside-down crucifixion of Peter, and tales of dragon-slaying, and try to explain the exclusion of certain accounts

programme.title: The Mystery of the Holy Lance
programme.desc: Mentioned in the Gospel of John, the Holy Lance is known as the weapon used by Roman legionary Longinus to pierce Christs side after his crucifixion. According to legend, whoever possesses it becomes invincible and capable of ruling the world. Throughout history, the Lance have been sought after by many ambitious leaders, such as Emperor Constantine, Napoleon, Barbarossa and Hitler. This documentary combines scientific investigation with the latest historical research to explore the 2,000 year-long history of this captivating artefact

programme.title: Book of Secrets with Lance Reddick
programme.desc: The host reveals that Nazi leader Adolf Hitler was so deeply into the occult, he established an organisation with a secret mission to track down and confiscate sacred relics. Lance also questions the success of the initiative

programme.title: Beyond Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: Notorious phenomenon-experiencer Chris Bledsoes daughter Emily purportedly has similar abilities to her father, so the team pools the duos talents at the Monroe Institute and the Florida Everglades

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: As the team launches high-powered rockets into the sky on Skinwalker Ranch, they are frozen in shock by what they encounter

programme.title: Death Coast
programme.desc: A powerful hurricane forces Jeff Mackinnon and his team into a secluded cove, where they investigate The Lombrac, an 18th-century ghost ship. As the divers recover clues from the depths, Jeff zeroes in on the site of a 300-year-old mystery that may be about to be solved

programme.title: Death Coast
programme.desc: With time running out, Jeff Mackinnon and his team return to Le Chameau for another attempt. Its members must make one final push to find the Chameaus remaining fortune and complete the mission Jeffs father started decades ago before time runs out

programme.title: Paranormal: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary the most chilling and unexplainable paranormal activity footage captured by eyewitnesses. A mysterious entity is spotted in the Irish countryside, a seance goes violently wrong and a UFO pays an overnight visit to the city that never sleeps

programme.title: Egypts Unexplained Files
programme.desc: New technology is employed to decode some of Egypts most enduring enigmas, to reveal the hidden truths that are rewriting human history. This episode explores the ancient Egyptian obsession with death and the afterlife, including the secrets behind Egyptians worship of the pyramid shape

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: UFO Over the Andes, the Ohio Grassman and Ancient Horn Mystery
programme.desc: The legendary Ohio Grassman is apparently captured in footage of a tall, dark-coloured, hairy creature walking through woods. Plus, a look at the Chilean family photograph of a real UFO flying above the Andes Mountains and the notion that a 5,000-year-old horn discovered on a beach in England belongs to a prehistoric creature that still lives

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Men in Black Calls, Old Kentucky Aliens and Mysterious Eclipse
programme.desc: Tony Harris and his team of experts examine several seemingly impossible events, such as a family managing to survive a bolt of lightning ripping through their car during a Kansas storm, a bizarre creature spotted on a driveway camera and an image captured of an alien probe

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Falcon 9 Mystery, Demonic Wall Climber and Leningrad Alien Fetus
programme.desc: A strange swirling vortex with a white centre seen in the skies over Oklahoma. Plus, a video said to be sealed in Vatican archives that shows a real case of demonic possession

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores Plymouths history during the Elizabethan era, visiting the home of the citys most famous son, Sir Francis Drake, and uncovering his history of piracy and slave trading - all carried out with approval from the Queen. She also discovers the revolutionary shipbuilding techniques that helped defeat the Spanish Armada, while aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson highlights the physical characteristics that made Plymouth Harbour such an attractive base for pirates

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts visits Portsmouth to investigate the history of the Royal Navy, uncovering some uncomfortable truths behind the myth surrounding Lord Horatio Nelson and getting first-hand experience of what life would have been like in the heat of battle at Trafalgar. She also visits an archaeological dig on Burrow Island and a lantern show at the New Theatre Royal, and discovers the brutal punishments doled out for even minor offences by the Victorian penal system

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team visit Wolverhampton, where a music box arrives for David Tupman to value, and letters from former prime ministers Edward Heath and Harold Macmillan land on Stewart Hofgartners table. David is also joined by Debbie Serpell and Karen Dalmeny, who haggle over their favourite items

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick and Corey dart out of the shop to check out a 1968 convertible. Chum wants a piece of an oreodont fossil, while Rick has quite an experience when a seller sends in a Jimi Hendrix poster. Later, a Harry and the Hendersons doll hits the counter

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly: Henrys Wreck-trospective

programme.title: Strange Evidence

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: A bizarre trend happened in Ireland in the 1980s when thousands of people started seeing religious statues move. Some residents of a village reported seeing a statue of the Virgin Mary move. Others believed the phenomenon was just an optical illusion or a prank

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Delving into the mysteries surrounding the death of Elvis Presley in 1977, investigating the theories that he remained alive, secretly, for some time afterwards

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: According to folklore, pots of gold and wishes are granted if a Leprechaun is caught. These mysterious little creatures are seemingly only found in Ireland. Alleged sightings of leprechauns and fairies are nothing new in Ireland. In fact, many claim they are part of everyday life on the Emerald Isle. Experts debate whether these Celtic creatures are fact or fiction

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: God moves in mysterious ways and sometimes appears in mysterious places. This episode lools at claims of mysterious religious sightings. Why do so many people see a vision of Jesus Christ in seemingly normal sights such as a tree stump or an antique cabinet?

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The patterns in fields, which appear overnight and without any warning. What are crop circles, why are they there and what could they possibly signify? Do they come from other worlds?

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The mystery surrounding a mass produced series of paintings of crying children, a popular artwork in many houses in the 1980s

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Investigating a strange creature seen in a wooded area of rural Massachusetts by three different people who all gave similar descriptions. Could the so-called Dover Demon be a never before discovered animal or could it have come from another civilisation?

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained

programme.title: The Simpsons
